verdict_reasoning: TGOLN2 (TGN46/TGN38 human ortholog) is a type-I single-pass TM protein that primarily resides in the trans-Golgi network but constitutively cycles between the TGN and the plasma membrane via secretory vesicles and endocytic recycling. Its ectodomain is exposed on the outer leaflet during the brief PM-residence phase of this cycle, and surface biotinylation / anti-TGN46 antibody uptake assays in intact cells routinely detect it at the cell surface. The dominant steady-state compartment is the TGN, but a documented minority PM pool exists via active vesicular cycling, which fits dual_localization. Not yes because surface residency is transient/minor; not no because the PM pool is well-documented.
